texts: They’re just kind of boned for the rest of the season apparently. They’ve said it’s a problem with the guns, but given the way f1 logistics work so far in advance it’s not clear they’ll be able to replace any of them before the seasons out. edit: to be clear I also think this is goofy, but that's the reason they're giving. Not sure if there's a weird cost cap/FIA rule that affects this, but that's what's being reported. The Race podcast, as u/Wingcapx noted.

Franz Tost and Christian Horner don't get enough credit for making this work. Think about that Pierre Gasly finish at 4th in Bahrain the first year Honda was with Toro Rosso. Toro Rosso bascially said we will build around the engine. Which is the polar opposite of McLaren and now AM. Mclaren wanted the size zero concept, and now with AM Newey, who already started late, had had re-design the batteries and placement late in the cycle. He also wanted the design as compact as possible.
The only way they were losing the WCC was if they had double DNFs for the last 8 races AND Ferrari grabbed 1-2 in all remaining races. The former is more probable than the latter.

Hamilton and Max did an extra pit stop, so they had fresher tyres for more of the race. Actually, Red Bull's deficit to everyone else in Barcelona was truly shocking. Max was basically at least 6 tenths slower per lap than Ferrari, Mercedes and McLaren.
The big upgrade package is new lug nuts.
There’s a happy Floridian here who now won’t have to drive 6 hours home starting at 7pm.
The only reason he didn’t do the nord 24 is because he made a big stink about it and didn’t do the program because he think he’s god gift to racing even if he’s barely above average

You forgot what happened on lap 1, and the fact it was a clean championship before abu dhabi, where both drivers got a favourable, yet dodgy call. Cant be corrupt if both drivers got calls for and against them. Im talking 1 sided benefits throughout the whole season
Does this also mean F2 could be a wet race? I cannot wait to watch it

We'll see it once we get to Spielberg and also Hungaroring. The rumours currently seem to range from 5hp to 10hp and the rumours are coming from the same outlet - no one else has confirmed that Ferrari will get their ADUO homologation for Austria.
He is always talking about Max on this dumb podcast. Unfortunately he is a disgraced driver and a loser, so his connection to Max is his main thing
Nothing in the rule book about putting a big block in
Poor Russel, it's like no one in that team wants him to win.
Max is a good guy who like to stick to facts without BS, drama and arrogance.
Inb4 the new upgrade package looks suspiciously like the new Corvette ZR1X

It also took them till 2020 to move the side crash structure forward of the sidepod inlets and to place the sidepod inlets as high as possible, which Ferrari did in 2017 and most other teams copied in 2018.
“Great Gasly” sounds much better
Agreed. If the deg at a track is low, then we need the hard tyre to be so slow that a race run on S and M is faster.

Can't say much with certainty right now because there are already updated regulations for 2027 that were approved today, but there is always a small delay between WMSC approval and new regs being published. The F1 commission already agreed on "minor aerodynamic and bodywork changes", but we won't know where we are until these updated regs are public. However, I haven't seen any reports about restricting freedoms in the SLM system specifically.
So we should except bright blue skies all weekend. Got it
Everybody happy! What a great story
Every second driver at Red Bull has had these problems, intentionally crashes or not.
That is absolutely bananas that they didn’t go
I had no idea Max spoke German!
thought the same thing for Leclerc and his 8 wins, I expected more since he only drove for Ferrari

Once you get stuck into the world of the technical side of F1, you quickly realise that there is pretty much no journalist that can actually explain how any upgrades work or offer any insight into what the teams are trying to achieve by bringing them. This isn't new, it's always been this way. Back when Autosport/Motorsport used to do big articles about updates the teams brought, the whole article would basically just boil down to each new part either adding downforce or doing "flow conditioning".
Gasly's car is going to remember it has a Mercedes engine now.
I feel like this is a cop out. If they really needed new guns they rush out a single case with guns overnight. They can solve this problem but they don’t feel like they need to.
Bottle flip❌ F1 car flip✅
Surely Hamilton will score every race this season!
